behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: request-builder + API client wiring. A unit test of a helper could miss dropped labels at the call boundary (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch this; a captured in-process fake client is sufficient (REAL-INFRA no). Correctness does not depend on model judgment (LLM-DEP no), and no multi-agent chain exists (MULTI-STEP no). Rejected higher level: behavior text is about request payload content, not live provider behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a fake API client that records outbound payload; invoke classify with a known taxonomy and assert every valid label is present in the sent request.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This validates response parsing + taxonomy guard at module boundary, not just pure mapping logic (MOCK-MISS). A fake API response with an invalid label can reproduce the real failure; no real network/OS needed (REAL-INFRA no). Model output quality is not under test; only defensive post-processing is (LLM-DEP no). Rejected Agentic/System: behavior text specifies deterministic fallback rule for unexpected values.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API client returning an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"); assert module output is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly a pure normalization determinism check on identical inputs. No cross-module handoff is required to expose failure (MOCK-MISS no boundary dependency), no real infra, and no real model call needed.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization with the same raw response fixture multiple times and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text requires repeated live AI API calls and consistency across real model-driven responses. A stub cannot expose failures tied to real provider variability/behavior (MOCK-HIDE yes). This is above System because correctness depends on actual model outputs over repeated calls (LLM-DEP yes). Not Workflow: each call is a single classification step, not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real classification calls for the same document/taxonomy; log provider raw responses and final labels; assert label changes only when corresponding provider response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at interaction of token counting/policy/config and dispatch gating (MOCK-MISS boundary interaction). In-process tokenizer/policy wiring plus fake client is enough to catch whether over-budget inputs are truncated/rejected before send; no real OS/network/subprocess needed (REAL-INFRA no). This does not depend on model semantic output (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System/Agentic: behavior text is pre-dispatch enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small token budget; pass an over-budget document; assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) and verify fake API client sees only compliant payload or no dispatch.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Needs real external API accounting; local mocks cannot reveal provider-side tokenization/usage-report mismatches (REAL-INFRA yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Agentic: behavior text is about usage metering, not classification quality or model judgment content. Not Workflow: single call measurement.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API call with representative document under configured budget; assert provider-reported usage fields are <= budget threshold.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is retry-orchestration across client wrapper, backoff policy, and error handling. A deterministic stub that returns rate-limit then success can reproduce it (REAL-INFRA no). No dependence on model output quality (LLM-DEP no), no multi-agent chain. Rejected System: behavior text does not require validating real network timing pathologies, only correct wait-and-retry control flow.
behaviors.test_description: Stub API client to return rate-limit error first, success second; use controllable clock/sleeper; assert module waits/backs off, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing error.
